name: Olocau Red Route: Sierra Calderona
name_original: El Penya Blanca — Running circular por la senda de Olocau
description: This moderate 3.6-mile (5.8 km) circular jogging route in Olocau offers 612 feet (186 metres) of elevation gain and scenic views towards Pen
region_intro: Jogging around Olocau offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Sierra Calderona Natural Park, known as the "green lung" of Valencia. The region features extensive forests, including cork oak trees, and varied terrain ranging from accessible paths to challenging trails with significant elevation gains. Runners can explore natural features such as ravines like the Barranco del Carraixet, panoramic mountain views, and natural water sources. Best jogging routes around Olocau The most popular jogging route is Olocau Red Route: Sierra Calderona, a 3.6 miles (5.8 km) trail that takes about 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route traverses diverse forest environments within the natural park. Another top favourite among local runners is Barranco del Carranxeit – Los Cañarizos loop from Olocau, a difficult 16.0 miles (25.7 km) path. This route explores the Barranco del Carraixet, offering a challenging experience through varied terrain. Local runners also love the Castillo del Real – View from the Castel Real loop from Olocau, a 7.2 miles (11.6 km) trail leading through forest tracks and past historical sites, often completed in about 1 hour 31 minutes. Jogging around Olocau is defined by extensive forests, ravines, and varied mountain terrain, with routes offering options for different ability levels from easy paths to challenging long-distance runs. The routes in Olocau are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 600 runners have used komoot to explore Olocau's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Olocau?
A: Olocau offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 40 different trails to explore. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ranging from easy paths to challenging long-distance runs within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Olocau?
A: Yes, Olocau has several routes ideal for beginners. While most trails are moderate to difficult, there are at least two easy routes available, perfect for those new to trail running or looking for a more relaxed jog. These paths often traverse accessible areas of the natural park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes in Olocau?
A: Jogging routes in Olocau feature highly varied terrain. You can expect to run through extensive forests, including cork oak trees, along ravines like the Barranco del Carraixet, and across mountainous areas with panoramic views. The paths range from well-maintained tracks to more rugged trails, offering diverse experiences for runners.

Q: Can I combine my run with exploring historical sites in Olocau?
A: Absolutely! Several running routes in Olocau integrate historical exploration. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32006779" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo del Real – View from the Castel Real loop from Olocau&lt;/a&gt; is a 7.2-mile trail that leads through forest tracks and past the ancient 11th-century fortress. Another option is a 6 km route to the 5th-century BC Iberian settlement of Puntal dels Llops, offering a blend of exercise and history.

Q: Are there any routes that feature natural water sources or geological formations?
A: Yes, Olocau's trails often incorporate natural water sources and unique geological features. The 'Fountains Route' is an easy 3.3 km circular walk that takes you past remarkable springs like the Font del Frare, the Font de la Salud, and the El Arquet aqueduct. Additionally, the 'Geological Route' is an 8 km circular path within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park, showcasing fascinating formations such as breccias, conglomerates, and marine fossils.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Olocau?
A: The running routes in Olocau are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Runners frequently praise the diverse forest environments, the chance to breathe fresh air amidst stunning natural beauty, and the variety of terrain that caters to different fitness levels. The extensive network of trails within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park is a particular highlight.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging running options in Olocau?
A: Yes, Olocau is well-suited for challenging and long-distance running. A significant portion of the available routes, 32 out of 43, are classified as difficult. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31922496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Carranxeit – Los Cañarizos loop from Olocau&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 16.0-mile (25.7 km) path that explores the Barranco del Carraixet, offering a demanding experience through varied terrain and significant elevation gains.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many of Olocau's running routes are circular, providing convenient starting and ending points. Examples include the 'Fountains Route' and the 'Geological Route', both designed as circular paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6913037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olocau Visitor Center – Penya Blanca loop from Olocau&lt;/a&gt; is another circular option, covering 15.4 km.

Q: What are some interesting natural landmarks or viewpoints I can see while running?
A: While running in Olocau, you can encounter several natural landmarks and viewpoints. The Sierra Calderona Natural Park itself offers panoramic mountain views. Some routes pass by unique geological formations, and you might even catch a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839906" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Olocau and the Cliffs of Cova del Cavall&lt;/a&gt;, providing stunning vistas of the region.

Q: Are there any caves or unique geological features to explore near the running trails?
A: Yes, the area around Olocau features several interesting caves and geological formations. While running, you might be close to sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4079679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Las Cuevas Calientes&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5352202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Inés Cave&lt;/a&gt;, or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2725733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Sacañé&lt;/a&gt;. The 'Geological Route' specifically highlights fascinating formations such as breccias, conglomerates, and marine fossils within the natural park.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Olocau?
A: Olocau's Mediterranean climate makes it suitable for outdoor activities like jogging year-round. However, the extensive forests and natural features of the Sierra Calderona Natural Park provide shade, making spring and autumn particularly pleasant. Even in summer, early mornings or late afternoons can be ideal, while winters are generally mild enough for comfortable running.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through ancient orchards or unique local landscapes?
A: Yes, some routes in Olocau offer glimpses into the local agricultural landscape and ancient features. For example, routes along the Barranco del Carraixet can showcase ancient carob trees and the local orchard environment, providing a unique cultural and natural experience during your run.
tips: In Olocau there are a number of excellent areas for hiking and trail running. The ascent to Peña Blanca on the south face of La Calderona allows us to see a dense pine forest, tortuous ravines and dry stone walls used to retain the earth on the slopes.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the El Penya Blanca — Running circular on the Olocau trail?
faqs.answer: This moderate jogging route is approximately 5.8 kilometers long. On average, it takes about 45 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating and an elevation gain of around 186 meters, this trail is suitable for runners with some basic fitness. While not overly strenuous, beginners might find some sections challenging. Olocau also offers easier, shorter circular routes if you're looking for a gentler introduction to trail running.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this running route?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this circular route around Olocau is varied. You can expect a mix of forest tracks and paths. Some sections might involve rocky ground, especially if the trail passes through areas typical of the Sierra Calderona Natural Park. You'll also encounter landscapes characterized by dry farming and dry stone constructions.

faqs.question: What can I see along the El Penya Blanca route?
faqs.answer: Along this route, you'll experience diverse scenery, including panoramic mountain vistas and pine forests. A key highlight is the Penya Blanca summit. The wider Olocau area is also rich in historical sites, such as the 11th-century Castillo del Real and an Iberian settlement, which you might glimpse or pass near on other local trails.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: While the exact trailhead for this specific route isn't detailed, Olocau has several well-signposted municipal routes. Generally, you can find parking in or around the town of Olocau, which serves as a common starting point for many local trails. Look for designated parking areas near the town center or at the beginning of known trail networks.

faqs.question: Is the El Penya Blanca — Running circular on the Olocau trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas where wildlife might be present or when passing through agricultural land. Be sure to carry water for your pet, particularly during warmer months.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to jog this route is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder, making for more comfortable running conditions. Summers can be very hot in Valencia, so if you run then, it's best to go early in the morning or late in the evening.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail or the Sierra Calderona Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need specific permits or to pay entrance fees to access the El Penya Blanca trail or other general routes within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning fire prevention and environmental protection, as you are in a natural park area.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route covers significant sections of other established trails. You will find yourself on parts of El Camí IT5TR7 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, Senda Olocau, Senda Font del Frare, and Camino de Olla, among others. This offers a chance to experience different facets of the local trail network.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints along the El Penya Blanca running route?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The route includes the Penya Blanca summit, which offers excellent panoramic views of the surrounding mountains and the diverse landscapes of Olocau. You'll find several spots along the trail to pause and enjoy the vistas.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for this moderate jogging trail?
faqs.answer: For this moderate jogging trail, wear comfortable running shoes with good grip, as the terrain can be varied. Light, breathable athletic clothing is recommended. Always bring sufficient water, especially on warmer days, and consider a small backpack for essentials like snacks, a phone, and sun protection (hat, sunscreen).
